🗒 ️Source & Validity Note: The reason codes listed in the table above have been extracted directly from the official XP Z12-012 Annexe A V1.3 Excel file published by the French tax authority ("Tableau des motifs de STATUTS" tab), as of the date this blog was written (May 2026). This saves you the effort of manually reading through the standard. The data is ready for you to use as-is when configuring your SAP system. However, the French tax authority may update, add, or remove codes in future versions of the standard. We recommend periodically checking the official portal ( see Section 8 ) to ensure your configuration stays up to date.

 

6. Step-by-Step Configuration

 

 SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition:Use Configuration activity ID 107322 (Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions) in your configuration environment instead of the transaction and SM30 view mentioned in the steps below. All other steps remain the same.

Prerequisites

  • You have access to the SAP system with authorization to maintain customizing views (or the configuration environment for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition).
  • You have downloaded the latest version of the XP Z12-012 standard from the tax authority portal (see Section 8 for instructions).

Steps 

Step 1: Access the configuration view

  • SAP ERP / SAP S/4HANA / SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Run transaction EDOC_FR_SI_REASONCOD, or run SM30 and enter view name EDOFRSICODMOTIFV
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: In your configuration environment, search for Configuration activity 107322 (Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions)

Step 2: Create new entries

  • Click "New Entries"
  • Enter the Reason Code, use the official code identifier from the XP Z12-012 Excel file (e.g., CMD_ERR, REF_ERR). The complete list with all valid codes is provided in Section 5 of this blog.
  • Enter the Reason Code Description; a meaningful label in your preferred language
  • Check the action checkboxes (Accept, Reject, PartAccept, Suspend, Dispute) based on the applicability matrix in Section 5. The ✓ marks in that table indicate which actions each reason code is officially valid for, as defined by the French tax authority standard.

Step 3: Save your entries

  • Click Save
  • The entries are stored in Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions (EDOFRSICODMOTIF)

7. Entering Reason Codes and Action Codes in your system

Now that you have configured the reason codes and, since the action codes are pre-delivered by SAP, the system is ready to capture this information from the user at the right moment.

7.1  Which Actions Are Available Depends on the Document Status

The actions available to you in the eDocument Cockpit (Accept, Reject, Dispute, Suspend and Partially Accept) depend on the current status of the electronic document. For example, a document in Suspended status allows you to trigger Accept, Reject, Dispute or Partially Accept. A document in Rejected status allows you to trigger Accept, Dispute, Suspend or Partially Accept. A document in Accepted status has no further actions available, as it is a final status.

To see the complete list of statuses and which actions are possible for each, you can check directly in the eDocument Cockpit:

  1. Select Domestic Supplier Invoice process
  2. Click Help
  3. Click the link Process Statuses for Domestic Supplier Invoice

This opens the built-in system documentation with full status and action matrix for the Domestic Supplier Invoice process.

7.2  Pop-up Behaviour When an Action Is Triggered

When an action is triggered for an electronic document in the eDocument Cockpit, such as Accept, Reject, Dispute, Suspend, or Partially Accept, the system displays a pop-up asking for relevant data (Reason Code, Action Code, or both) based on your configuration.

If you have configured reason codes for that action, the pop-up will ask for both reason code and an action code. If no reason codes are configured for that action, the pop-up will ask only for an action code.

For example: if you click Dispute but have not configured any reason codes for the Dispute action, the pop-up will show only the Action Code field. It will not ask for a Reason Code. Once you configure reason codes for Dispute (as described in Section 3), the pop-up will show both fields.

8. Where to Find the Official Code Lists (XP Z12-012)

The official reason codes and action codes are published by the French tax authority as part of the XP Z12-012 standard. Follow these steps to download the official Excel file:

  1. Go to: https://www.impots.gouv.fr/specifications-externes-b2b
  2. Navigate to the AFNOR standard page for XP Z12-012

     

     3. Click "Consultation gratuite" (Free Consultation)

     4.Select "Consulter en Anglais" (or French)

     5. Click "Téléchargement des annexes" (Download Annexes)

     6. Extract the ZIP file. It contains the Excel file XP_Z12-012_Annexe_A_2026_V1.3.xlsx

     7. Open the Excel file and navigate to:

  • "Codes Action" tab → Action codes (already pre-delivered by SAP; for reference only)
  • "Tableau des motifs de STATUTS" tab → Reason codes matrix with applicable statuses (use this to configure Section 3)

9. Key Takeaways

Key Takeaways Table

Topic

Summary

Reason Codes

Must be configured by the customer using 'Activate Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions (transaction EDOC_FR_SI_REASONCOD)'. Use the XP Z12-012 standard ( Section 8 ) as your reference.

Action Codes

Pre-delivered by SAP. No configuration needed. Aligned with the official XP Z12-012 standard.

Pop-up behavior

Shows Reason Code + Action Code fields if reason codes are configured for the action; shows only Action Code otherwise.

Reject & Tax Authority

Only the Reject action status is transmitted to the French tax authority (DGFiP), this is a legal mandate. All other actions are communicated only to the sender access point.

Maintenance

The tax authority may update the reason code list. Monitor the XP Z12-012 standard and update your SAP configuration accordingly.

 

10. Related SAP Help Portal Documentation

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Processing Electronic Domestic Supplier Invoices
  • Activating Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Processing Electronic Domestic Supplier Invoices
  • Activating Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions

SAP ERP

  • Processing Electronic Domestic Supplier Invoices
  • Activating Reason Codes for Supplier Invoice Actions

I hope you find this information useful. You can leave a comment on this blog or follow us for more information about SAP Document and Reporting Compliance here in SAP Community.

Author: Mahesh Varma Kalidindi | SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

entry.title: Agentic Joule for developer: It’s all about YOUR CHOICE
entry.content:
At Sapphire, SAP made one theme impossible to miss: the future is agentic, the ERP is autonomous, and the SAP Business AI platform is turning into the foundation for how this intelligence gets delivered across the portfolio. Joule for Developers is part of that bigger story.

While some technology announcements feel like feature updates, this one feels more like a launch moment — the point where the plot twist. Over the past year, ABAP AI has been proving its value in the practical trenches: explaining old code, accelerating test work, helping with findings, and making cloud development easier. Useful? Absolutely. But still mostly assistive. What Sapphire makes clear is that the next chapter is far more ambitious: ABAP AI is evolving from a set of smart skills into an agent-led development model — one built around choice: your choice of your preferred IDE between ABAP Development Tools on Eclipse or on VSCode, your partner of choice with your preferred AI assistant and orchestration, your choice of ABAP system from BTP till any S/4HANA version from 2021 up and your choice of consumption. And on top a much more serious strategy for RISE custom code migration.

That is the real shift here. Not just “more AI,” but AI that starts behaving like a coordinated system.

So this blog is not about one more feature. This one is about a full shift of thinking, with the upcoming general availability of the ABAP MCP server, open support for partner assistants and orchestrators, the general availability of ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code, a new consumption-based commercial model, and major progress in custom code migration, SAP Joule for Developers is turning into a developer platform built around one central idea: your choice.

Agentic Joule for Developers arrives with the upcoming general availability of the ABAP MCP server and an open partner strategy built around your choice of assistants and orchestrators

The most exciting signal in this story is the ABAP MCP server as of this quarter: Q2 2026. It opens ABAP development to a wider agentic universe by exposing ABAP capabilities in a way that assistants and orchestrators can truly work with. This is where the story becomes more than assistance and starts to feel like momentum. Instead of one closed experience, SAP is shaping a future in which customers and partners can bring their preferred AI assistant, their preferred orchestration layer, and their preferred model strategy into the flow. The result is a far more elegant promise for ABAP teams: SAP provides the ABAP intelligence, the MCP layer becomes the bridge, and you decide how the full agentic experience comes together.

ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code completes the promise of real IDE choice for ABAP developers

Choice becomes real the moment it reaches the daily developer experience, and that is exactly why ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code matters so much. It turns SAP’s commitment to IDE freedom into something tangible. Teams can stay in the environment that feels natural to them while still tapping into the full power of agentic ABAP. In this new model, Visual Studio Code becomes the creative workspace, the MCP layer becomes the action engine, and partner orchestration brings the journey together from first intent to finished Fiori application. It is a smoother, more modern, more natural way to build. For the first release the new Visual Studio Code is focused on ABAP Cloud but by the end of the year with the promise of the roadmap, the entire ABAP Classic support will be extended from Eclipse to VSCode IDE also.

Meeting you where you are with ABAP AI across private cloud, public cloud, on premise, and BTP

One of the most expected announcement for Joule for developer was not on the features but on the reach: the customers stretch across private cloud, public cloud, BTP and either on premise systems and starting June, Joule for Developers is being positioned to meet our customers where they are, across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP BTP ABAP Environment, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition releases from 2021 onward. More than that for the RISE customers who fulfill the conditions, Joule for Developer will be available also on their On-Premise systems from 2021 onward. This changes the tone of adoption completely. Instead of waiting for every landscape decision to be perfect, teams can begin from the reality they already have and move forward from there.

Custom code migration steps into a new era with an agent-led toolchain for SAP S/4HANA transformation

If there is one place where agentic AI has to prove it can deliver more than clever assistance, it is custom code migration. And the CCM story now reads much more clearly as an end-to-end journey. It starts upstream with Cloud ALM, where project analysis helps teams understand the migration scope, structure the backlog, and identify where effort and risk are concentrated. That early project view matters because migration is never just a developer task. It is a transformation program. But once the scope is understood, the story shifts decisively into developer execution, and that is where the three CCM execution agents provided by Joule for developer come in.



1. Mass S/4 Custom Code Conversion Agent
This first agent planned for Q2 2026 is about scale. It helps developers take large volumes of custom code findings and move from assessment into executable remediation. Its role is to accelerate the mass conversion work that would otherwise remain trapped in analysis reports and manual follow-up.

2. Clean Core Adoption Agent
This second agent is about clean core transformation. It helps teams to move from technical correction to clean-core through recommendation and automatic fixes. This is planned for Q3 and Q4 2026.

3. Redesign and Modernization Agent (i.e. Dynpro-to-RAP)
This third and most complex agent is about application modernization and redesign. It focuses on the deeper transformation work, helping classic Dynpro-based patterns evolve toward a RAP-based future and turning modernization into a guided execution flow rather than a one-off rebuild effort. This agent is planned on the roadmap for late this year and early next year.

Together, these three agents create a much more structured CCM storyline: analyze the program in Cloud ALM, then execute the migration through conversion, clean-core adoption, and modernization. The timeline reinforces that progression. The first execution step begins in Q2 2026, broader execution capabilities expand through the second half of 2026, and the larger modernization promise continues to mature into 2027. What emerges is not just a better migration tool, but a much more credible migration motion from project analysis to developer delivery.

A new commercial model that lets you choose what you use, when you use it, and how far you scale it

Agentic Joule for developer is built as a choice-driven ecosystem also at the commercial layer. The model is designed for flexibility with consumption-based pricing, clear J4D activation, and differentiated paths for customers and partners. While the free promotion will end on 30th September the teams can work through ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse or VS Code, connect through the ABAP MCP Server and local ABAP MCP tools, at no extra cost, while taping into an agent pipeline powered by an open partner strategy at your choice that includes Microsoft, Amazon, Google, IBM, Anthropic and more. The commercial message is bold and unmistakable: Agentic Joule for developer is not just offering AI capabilities — it is giving customers and partners the freedom to build their own path, with their own tools, their own partners, and their own pace, paying only for the AI features which truly bring value to them. It feels far better suited to the rhythm of real AI adoption, where teams experiment, learn, expand, and refine over time rather than locking themselves into a single path on day one.

Bottom line: the future of ABAP looks less like a chatbot and more like an ecosystem

Put it all together and one message shines through powerfully: Agentic Joule for Developers is becoming a platform shaped around your choice. Your assistant. Your orchestrator. Your IDE. Your landscape. Your migration path. Your pace. And that is exactly what makes this new chapter feel so powerful: ABAP AI is growing up — from answering questions to orchestrating work, from isolated skills to partner-powered ecosystems, and from code assistance to full custom code migration strategy.

entry.title: Why SAP Signavio Reveals Process Issues That Traditional SAP Projects Often Miss
entry.content:
Why SAP Signavio Reveals Process Issues That Traditional SAP Projects Often Miss

Introduction

One lesson I have learned from working on SAP transformation programs is that technology problems are often easier to solve than process problems.

Most SAP projects invest significant effort in system configuration, data migration, testing, and cutover planning. These activities are critical for a successful implementation. However, many organizations still encounter operational challenges after go-live, even when the technical deployment itself is successful.

In many cases, the root cause is not the SAP system. It is the underlying business process.

Over the years, I have seen situations where different regions followed different approval paths, procurement processes varied between business units, and operational teams developed workarounds that were never formally documented. These variations often remain hidden until transformation programs attempt to standardize processes across the organization.

This is where SAP Signavio can provide significant value.

The Challenge with Traditional Process Discovery

Historically, process discovery relied heavily on workshops, interviews, documentation reviews, and process flow diagrams.

While these methods remain important, they also have limitations.

Business users may describe how a process is supposed to work rather than how it actually works. Documentation may be outdated. Regional teams may follow different procedures despite having the same documented process.

As a result, organizations often design future-state processes based on assumptions rather than actual execution data.

When these assumptions prove incorrect, issues begin to surface during testing, user adoption, or after go-live.

How SAP Signavio Changes the Conversation

What makes SAP Signavio different is its ability to analyze real operational data and provide visibility into how processes are actually executed across the organization.

Instead of relying solely on interviews and workshops, process mining helps organizations identify:

  • Process bottlenecks

  • Approval delays

  • Rework activities

  • Process variations

  • Compliance deviations

  • Manual workarounds

  • Inefficient handoffs between teams

This creates a fact-based view of operational reality rather than a theoretical view of how processes should work.

In my experience, this visibility often uncovers opportunities that traditional process assessments fail to identify.

Traditional Process Discovery vs Process Mining with SAP Signavio

Example: Procurement Process Variations

In one transformation initiative, procurement processes appeared standardized on paper.

However, when transaction data was analyzed, significant differences emerged across regions.

Some purchase requests moved through three approval levels while others required six. Certain business units consistently bypassed automated workflows and relied on manual interventions. Lead times varied considerably despite similar business requirements.

Without process mining, these variations would likely have remained hidden until much later in the transformation program.

By identifying these differences early, the organization was able to simplify approval paths, reduce unnecessary complexity, and establish more consistent operating procedures.

Illustrative Procurement Process Variations Identified Through SAP Signavio

Illustrative example showing how SAP Signavio process mining can identify approval bottlenecks, process variations, and manual workarounds across regions. Sample process flows are provided for educational purposes only.

Why Process Standardization Matters

One of the primary goals of many SAP S/4HANA programs is process standardization.

Organizations often seek to:

  • Improve efficiency

  • Reduce operational risk

  • Increase compliance

  • Simplify support models

  • Improve reporting consistency

  • Enable scalable growth

However, standardization becomes difficult when the organization lacks visibility into existing process variations.

SAP Signavio helps establish that visibility.

By understanding how work actually flows through the business, organizations can make informed decisions about which processes should be standardized and where flexibility remains necessary.

Supporting Successful S/4HANA Transformations

Many organizations view process mining as a separate initiative.

I believe its greatest value comes when it is integrated directly into SAP transformation programs.

When used early in the project lifecycle, SAP Signavio can help teams:

  • Establish process baselines

  • Identify improvement opportunities

  • Reduce process complexity

  • Improve business readiness

  • Support change management activities

  • Increase confidence in future-state designs

This reduces the risk of discovering major process issues during testing or after go-live.

SAP Signavio Supporting SAP S4HANA Transformation Programs

Illustrative example showing how SAP Signavio process mining supports SAP S/4HANA transformation programs by identifying bottlenecks, enabling process optimization, improving compliance, and driving better business outcomes.

Beyond Technology

One misconception is that SAP transformations fail because of technology limitations.

In reality, many transformation challenges originate from process inconsistencies, unclear ownership, or operational inefficiencies that existed long before the project began.

Technology can enable improvement, but it cannot automatically correct broken processes.

Tools such as SAP Signavio help organizations understand those processes before large-scale changes are introduced.

That understanding often becomes one of the most valuable outcomes of the transformation itself.

Conclusion

From my experience, some of the most important discoveries in SAP transformation programs come from understanding how the business actually operates rather than how it believes it operates.

SAP Signavio provides organizations with a data-driven approach to process visibility, helping uncover bottlenecks, variations, and inefficiencies that traditional assessments may overlook.

As organizations continue investing in SAP S/4HANA and digital transformation initiatives, process transparency will become increasingly important. The earlier process issues are identified, the easier they are to address, and the greater the likelihood of achieving sustainable transformation outcomes.
